name: Scrambled “egg” and Bread with Sweet Tea
intro: This simple breakfast is loved by many across Nigeria, and was a firm favourite when I was growing up. Buttered bread and tea is a standard way to start the day when in a hurry, and it is common practice to dip the bread into the steaming hot sweet tea. The addition of scramble makes this breakfast extra filling and provides a protein boost. For this vegan version, we will use tofu to replace the eggs. Any type of bread will do, but if you really want to eat like a Nigerian, use Agege bread (see page 41 for the recipe or buy a fresh loaf from your local African grocery store).

ingredients.item: scotch bonnet chilli (deseeded and finely chopped)

method: Heat the sunflower oil in a frying pan and add the onion, red pepper and chilli. Fry on medium heat for about 5 minutes until the vegetables soften. Crumble the tofu into the frying pan and season with curry powder, turmeric and salt to taste. Mix and stir-fry for another 5 minutes until the tofu is heated through.
Prepare your black tea as you normally would, adding as much plant-based milk and/or sugar as you care for.
Serve the scramble alongside a chunk of Agege bread and a steaming mug of sweet tea.
